commit.message:
feat(podcast-pal): replace xelatex PDF pipeline with platform pdf skill (#80)

Remove all LaTeX/xelatex machinery from the podcast-pdf skill and replace
with instructions to use the pre-loaded platform pdf skill. Content quality
rules (thematic organization, direct quotes, highlight boxes, figures,
bilingual support) are preserved, re-expressed in Markdown instead of LaTeX.

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix generate_collage.py fallback: emit path, not image_url (#86)

The designer CLI writes the generated image to a local file and prints the
file path on stdout (not a URL). After the CLI rename, the fallback branch
was storing that local path under the "image_url" key, which callers would
try to use as a remote URL.

Match the sibling make_product_grid.py convention: when the value is a local
path, use the "path" key; reserve "image_url" for the primary aimage_generation
path whose return value is a real URL. Callers must now check both fields.

Update outfit-recommendation/SKILL.md Step 7 to document the two return shapes.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author tim-srp <tim@srp.one> 1776334736 +0800
committer GitHub <noreply@github.com> 1776334736 +0800

The designer CLI writes the generated image to a local file and prints the

Match the sibling make_product_grid.py convention: when the value is a local
path, use the "path" key; reserve "image_url" for the primary aimage_generation

Update outfit-recommendation/SKILL.md Step 7 to document the two return shapes.

commit.message:
Align designer CLI usage and fix hardcoded /extra-skills paths (#85)

After fastclaw PR #56 remapped the ecap-skills mount point, the hardcoded
/extra-skills/... paths no longer resolve. In addition, the designer skill's
image_generation_cli.py renamed --image-urls to --images and changed stdout
from JSON to a plain file path.

Changes:
- Replace --image-urls with --images across all bash examples and docs
  (stylist-agent, soulmate-pack)
- Replace /extra-skills/<skill>/... with ~/.openclaw/skills/<skill>/...
  so the path resolves via $HOME at runtime
  (bash blocks use tilde expansion; Python subprocess uses os.path.expanduser)
- Update generate_collage.py fallback_generate() to parse the new plain
  stdout format and widen its return type to str | None

Affected agents: stylist-agent, soulmate-pack, restaurant-review-monitor,
zoodance-vibe-drama.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

parent 440dde63bfce0bdeac8d09842d6ed2249b797752
author tim-srp <tim@srp.one> 1776332786 +0800
committer GitHub <noreply@github.com> 1776332786 +0800

zoodance-vibe-drama.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
chore: remove obsolete /extra-skills path references from prompts and docs (#84)

* chore: remove obsolete /extra-skills path references from prompts and docs

ecap-skills have been remounted at ~/.openclaw/skills/ (managed priority
level 3) by fastclaw PR #56, which naturally overrides bundled skills.
The old "Tool Routing Policy (Extra Skills First)" prompt hack and the
hardcoded /extra-skills/ path references in docs are no longer needed.

Changes:
- Remove "Tool Routing Policy" section from 5 AGENTS.md files
  (careerbloom, oura-ring-connector, podcast-pal,
   restaurant-review-monitor, stylist-agent)
- Drop Path column from design-researcher/TOOLS.md skill table
- Replace /extra-skills/<name> text references with skill names in
  dr-report-builder/SKILL.md, mkt-meta-ads-pro/SKILL.md, stylist-agent/TOOLS.md

Out of scope (follow-up):
- bash blocks in SKILL.md that invoke `python3 /extra-skills/...`
- Python scripts with hardcoded subprocess paths

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: update subprocess script paths for ecap-skills managed-priority mount

After fastclaw PR #56, ecap-skills are mounted at ~/.openclaw/skills/
(managed priority 3) instead of /extra-skills/. Update the cross-skill
subprocess calls in these scripts so they resolve the correct location.

Using os.path.expanduser('~/...') keeps the code independent of the
container's HOME user (currently 'node') without hardcoding
/home/node/.openclaw in sources.

Files:
- restaurant-review-monitor/skills/daily-digest/scripts/fetch_yelp.py
- restaurant-review-monitor/skills/daily-digest/scripts/run_daily.py
- stylist-agent/skills/outfit-recommendation/scripts/generate_collage.py

Rationale for keeping subprocess pattern:
- run_daily.py and fetch_yelp.py run as an OpenClaw cron job with no
  LLM in the loop, so "let the corresponding skill do it" via agent
  routing is not applicable. subprocess is the current canonical
  cron-to-skill invocation mechanism.
- generate_collage.py's subprocess call is a fallback path only; the
  primary path uses litellm directly. Rewriting the fallback would
  change the script's exception-handling contract.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Revert "chore: update subprocess script paths for ecap-skills managed-priority mount"

This reverts commit eab1244d83013be9a57f73d94d38634d369c3425.

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(oura-ring-connector, podcast-pal): move config to ~/.config for persistence across claw restarts (#83)

Relocate all runtime data paths from ~/.<pack-name>/ to ~/.config/<pack-name>/
so that config, auth tokens, and cached data survive container restarts.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
